newsSinceJun6: Apollo Global Management withdrew its 885p/share all-cash proposal (~GBP 1.52B / $2.04B) on Friday June 5; shares fell ~13% and the entire bid premium has now unwound (pre-bid undisturbed close ~697p vs 708.5p today, +0.43% on the day). Apollo is barred from re-approaching for six months under the Takeover Code. The board reiterated FY2026 guidance and its 'Optimise, Perform and Grow' independent strategy, citing a positive start to 2026 trading (AGM update May 27). No reason given for Apollo's withdrawal.

sources.label: Reuters via Investing.com - Apollo bows out of $2 billion takeover pursuit of UK's Bodycote
RTTNews - Bodycote Shares Down After Apollo Withdraws Offer
ADVFN - Bodycote Backs Independent Growth Plan After Apollo Ends Takeover Interest

verdicts.rankRationale: Sharp deal-break sell-off (-13% Friday, full bid premium unwound to roughly the undisturbed price) with the thermal-processing thesis untouched improves asymmetry on a latent-node alpha list; a sophisticated PE buyer just marked intrinsic value at 885p, ~25% above today's 708.5p. Yesterday's 'down' was a mechanical rank reconciliation that predates incorporating this setup, so this is genuinely new evidence, not a reversal on noise.
verdicts.bull: Apollo's withdrawn 885p bid is a hard valuation marker ~25% above the current price, FY2026 guidance is intact with a positive trading start, and the AI-relevant thermal-processing capability (liquid-cooling loops, robotics-grade metal treatment) is unpriced at a flat YTD, $1.6B name.
verdicts.neutral: The stock has merely round-tripped to its undisturbed pre-bid level; without a new bidder or an explicit AI-revenue proof point, it trades as a cyclical UK industrial and the latent-node thesis stays optionality, not earnings.
verdicts.bear: Apollo walked without explanation after diligence, which may signal margin or end-market issues it found; with the suitor barred for six months and no near-term catalyst, the shares can drift lower on deal-arb exit flows and geopolitical/industrial-demand softness.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A second bidder emerging or H1 2026 results (late July) evidencing the positive trading start and any AI/data-center-adjacent thermal-processing wins.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Apollo's unexplained walk-away reflects fundamental diligence concerns, and post-deal-break flow pressure pushes the stock below the undisturbed price.
